title: PROP-FEED-S5-004 'feed_initial_state integration test' does not specify the test fixture (temp dir? mock filesystem? what vault state?), making the obligation under-determined
description:
PROP-FEED-S5-004 is a Tier-1 obligation discharged by a cargo integration test. The verification-architecture.md prescribes test names (lines 720-722: `test_feed_initial_state_empty_vault`, `test_feed_initial_state_existing_one_note`, `test_feed_initial_state_no_file_created`) but does not specify: (a) whether the test uses a real temp directory (`tempfile::tempdir`?) or some test harness; (b) how the test seeds 'an existing note' (line 721) — does it write a real `.md` file? if so, with what frontmatter? (the `parse_frontmatter_metadata` function will produce different results depending on whether the file has a YAML header); (c) how the Clock is fixed for determinism (line 942 of the spec mentions 'Clock 固定 + 既存 ID 1 件' for the collision test, but the test strategy at lines 718 says 'fixed Clock' without explaining the mechanism — `feed_initial_state` calls `SystemTime::now()` internally per REQ-FEED-028 step 3, so 'fixing the clock' requires either dependency injection or a global override that the spec has not introduced); (d) how the 'no file created' assertion is checked between calls (line 944 says 'std::fs::read_dir で確認' — but the file count is checked before vs. after by what mechanism? a pre-test snapshot?). 

The practical consequence: the implementer has to invent a fixture pattern not authorized by the spec, and the adversary cannot tell from the spec whether a proposed implementation discharges the obligation. Tier-1 means 'integration test must exist and pass' — but if the test fixture is unspecified, the test can be written to test only a degenerate case (e.g., empty vault with the system clock — non-deterministic for ID format AC). The PROP must specify the fixture contract.
evidence:
verification-architecture.md:709 — 'cargo test (Rust integration test) | true | shell'
verification-architecture.md:720-722 — test names listed but fixtures unspecified
verification-architecture.md:694 — pure helper signature is decoupled from clock; but the integration test exercises `feed_initial_state` which calls `SystemTime::now()` directly per REQ-FEED-028 step 3
behavioral-spec.md:942 — 'Clock 固定 + 既存 ID 1 件' (fixed clock) — but no mechanism for fixing the clock in the impure shell is introduced
remediation: Specify in §14 Sprint 5 Rust Test Strategy: (a) the test fixture uses `tempfile::tempdir()` to create an isolated vault, (b) the 'existing note' is seeded by writing a fixed `.md` file with a known stem (e.g., `2020-01-01-000000-000.md`) and known frontmatter, (c) the collision test fixes the wall clock either by passing `vault_path` whose existing file matches the format that `SystemTime::now()` would generate (impractical — the test would be flaky), or by refactoring `feed_initial_state` to accept an injected `Clock` trait object (this is a real spec gap), (d) the 'no file created' check compares `read_dir` output before vs. after via an explicit count assertion. Without these specifics, PROP-FEED-S5-004 is not falsifiable as a single contract.
